_extract_meta.purpose: Researcher-directed extract: terms identified as clearly belonging in other clusters, for CC reallocation. Terms with marginal or split placement (H7230 rov, H1878 da.shen, H3520B ke.vud.dah) are excluded — researcher decision pending.
open_questions_for_researcher: OQ-R01: H1433 go.del — VCGs split across M22 (649-001) and M08 (649-002). Confirm rebind to M22 as primary, or split the term across two clusters?
OQ-R02: G2770 kerdainō — M37 Calling or M36 Service as destination? M37 preferred in this extract.
OQ-R03: H4766 mar.veh — M33 Peace or M18 Hope as destination? Both are defensible.
OQ-R04: H4768 mar.bit — confirm M23 Strength as destination, or consider FLAG given extracted_thin status?

existing_vcgs.description: Go.del as greatness of God as basis of prayer, worship, and covenant confidence
Go.del as pride or self-exalting greatness subject to divine judgment

data_quality_flags: flag_id=3: Low occurrence count (13). Statistical patterns unreliable below 20.
flag_id=4: meaning field is null. STEP returned no word analysis block.

reallocation.primary_rationale: Group 649-001 (greatness of God as basis of prayer, worship, and covenant confidence) is doxological vocabulary — it names the greatness of God as the ground of worship. M22 Praise/Thanksgiving/Glory is the natural home: it contains glory (ka.vod), praise (te.hil.lah), to praise (ha.lal), to give thanks (ya.dah), to honor (ha.dar). The Deut/Num/Psa verses in 649-001 are all doxological or intercessory in register.
reallocation.secondary_rationale: Group 649-002 (pride or self-exalting greatness subject to divine judgment) is pride vocabulary — Isa 9:9 (arrogance of heart), Isa 10:12 (arrogant heart of the king of Assyria), Eze 31:2/7/18 (Pharaoh's greatness as pride). M08 Pride/Arrogance/Boasting is the correct home: it contains arrogance (za.don), pride (ga.a.vah, ga.on), haughtiness, to exalt (rum).
reallocation.note: This term has VCGs split across two different destination clusters. CC will need to handle both VCG migrations and the mti_terms.cluster_code rebind. Suggest researcher decision on whether to rebind to M22 (majority VCG) or split — or whether go.del should be duplicated across both clusters. Most likely: rebind to M22 as primary, with 649-002 noted as cross-cluster. Researcher to confirm.

existing_vcgs.description: Ge.dul.lah as greatness of God as object of worship and doxological declaration
existing_vcgs.verse_status: G (9 verses) + 2 UT (Est 1:4 confirmed relevant; Est 6:3 confirmed relevant)

data_quality_flags: flag_id=3: Low occurrence count (15). Statistical patterns unreliable below 20.
flag_id=4: meaning field is null. STEP returned no word analysis block.

reallocation.primary_rationale: The dominant VCG (6903-001) is explicitly doxological: 2Sa 7:21/23, 1Ch 17:19/21, 1Ch 29:11, Psa 71:21, Psa 145:3/6, Est 10:2 — all name the greatness of God as the object of worship and doxological declaration. M22 is the correct home. The two UT-now-relevant verses (Est 1:4 — royal pride; Est 6:3 — honor/distinction as moral category) are secondary instances that do not shift the dominant register.
reallocation.note: Est 1:4 (royal display of greatness) could be argued for M08 Pride, but since the single VCG is overwhelmingly doxological, rebind to M22 is appropriate. Est 1:4 and Est 6:3 will follow the VCG to M22 and can be handled there.

existing_vcgs.description: Luxury and delight as the inner orientation toward what is deeply pleasing

data_quality_flags: flag_id=3: Low occurrence count (5). Statistical patterns unreliable below 20.
flag_id=4: meaning field is null. STEP returned no word analysis block.

reallocation.primary_rationale: Ta.a.nug (luxury/delight) is rooted in the ANOG root family: related to a.nog (to delight, H6026), o.neg (delight, H6027), a.nog (dainty, H6028). M04 Joy/Gladness/Delight already contains dainty (a.nog), delight (o.neg), pleasure (che.phets), pleasantness (no.am) — the exact semantic neighbourhood. The origin registry is R042 delight (legacy C03 = Joy cluster). The VCG description ('luxury and delight as inner orientation toward what is deeply pleasing') is Joy/Delight vocabulary. M46 Abundance is the wrong home; M04 is the natural and correct one.

existing_vcgs.description: Term names greatness, majority, or abundance as a quality of persons or communities — the inner being engaged through loyalty, amazement, or spiritual state

data_quality_flags: flag_id=3: Low occurrence count (5). Statistical patterns unreliable below 20.
flag_id=4: meaning field is null. STEP returned no word analysis block.

reallocation.primary_rationale: Mar.bit functions as a majority/proportion/greatness quantifier for groups of persons. The REVU root family connects it to ra.vah (to multiply, H7235A), tar.but (increase, H8635), tar.bit (increment, H8636) — quantitative increase vocabulary. The origin registry R197 authority sits in the legacy C20 strength cluster. M23 Strength/Power/Dominion is the receiving home: it contains growing (ga.del), dominion vocabulary, and collective-strength terms. The inner-being content (loyalty of the Benjaminites, majority who ate Passover wrongly, Solomon's wisdom scale) is secondary to the quantitative-group function. This is the better fit than M46 Abundance.
reallocation.note: extracted_thin status: only 5 occurrences, 5 verse records. Analytical conclusions must be proportionally limited regardless of destination. Researcher may also consider whether this term warrants FLAG status given thinness.

existing_vcgs.description: Term names goodness as an inner-being disposition and Spirit-produced fruit — a quality that fills the person and is completed by God, expressed in righteous conduct and resolve

data_quality_flags: flag_id=3: Only 4 confirmed verse records. Threshold is 5.
flag_id=4: meaning field is null. STEP returned no word analysis block.
flag_id=47: Meaning stored as single prose block. No structured sense numbering.

NOTE: This term has vc_status=vc_completed and md_version=2 — it has been fully analysed under R067 goodness with 140+ findings attached. The existing analytical record is extensive and must be preserved in the receiving cluster.

reallocation.primary_rationale: Agathōsunē is a Spirit-produced inner-being virtue (Gal 5:22, Rom 15:14, Eph 5:9, 2Th 1:11) — goodness as the fruit of the Spirit and the disposition that fills the person. M39 Blessing/Favour/Grace already contains: be good (ya.tav, te.ev), be pleasing (tov), goodwill (eudokia), grace (charis), to bless (ba.rakh), to favour (charitoō). Agathōsunē belongs in this register — it is a grace/goodness/Spirit-gift term, not an abundance/prosperity term. M46 Abundance is structurally mismatched for this term's inner-being role.
reallocation.note: md_version=2 and vc_completed status: this term carries the full R067 goodness analytical record including 14 direct findings and 126 via-anchor findings. CC must ensure this record migrates intact or remains accessible after rebind. Recommend verifying that cluster_finding rows linked to this term are not disrupted by the cluster_code change.

existing_vcgs.description: Term names the inner-being goal of winning others — the motivating purpose behind Paul's self-subordination; love for others expressed as the drive to gain them for salvation

data_quality_flags: flag_id=3: Only 4 confirmed verse records. Threshold is 5.
flag_id=4: meaning field is null. STEP returned no word analysis block.
flag_id=47: Meaning stored as single prose block. No structured sense numbering.

reallocation.primary_rationale: Kerdainō in 1Cor 9:19-22 names Paul's inner-being drive to win people for salvation — 'that I might win more of them', 'that I might win Jews', 'that I might win those outside the law'. This is missional motivation and vocation: the inner purpose of self-subordination for the sake of others' salvation. M37 Calling/Election/Vocation (to call, to choose, ba.char) is the most appropriate destination. The term names the inner goal of apostolic calling — gaining people for Christ — which is a calling/vocation register, not an abundance/prosperity register.
reallocation.alternative_destination: M36 Service, Slavery and Labour — kerdainō's mechanism (making oneself a servant to all, 1Cor 9:19) overlaps with M36 Service vocabulary. Either M37 or M36 is defensible; M37 is preferred as the motivational-vocation register is primary.
reallocation.note: Researcher to confirm M37 vs M36 if preferred alternative differs.

existing_vcgs.description: Term names the increasing scope of God's government and peace — the inner being engaged through messianic hope
existing_vcgs.verse_status: G (Isa 9:7 only; Isa 33:23 was UT — set aside in Phase 2)

data_quality_flags: flag_id=3: Only 2 confirmed verse records. Threshold is 5.
flag_id=4: meaning field is null. STEP returned no word analysis block.

NOTE: Isa 33:23 (the only other verse) was set aside in Phase 2 UT review. Effectively this term rests on a single verse (Isa 9:7) with existing via-anchor findings from R117 peace analysis (5 findings).

reallocation.primary_rationale: The sole active verse is Isa 9:7: 'Of the increase of his government and of peace there will be no end.' The VCG description itself names 'the increasing scope of God's government and peace — inner being engaged through messianic hope.' The 5 via-anchor findings from R117 peace already treat this verse as a peace/eschatological text. The REVU root family (same as H4768 mar.bit) connects mar.veh to ra.vah (to multiply), tar.but/tar.bit (increase). The term names the increase/abundance of peace — its inner-being content is messianic peace and hope. M33 Peace/Rest/Quietness is the natural home. M18 Hope/Expectation/Waiting is an alternative if the messianic-hope register is primary over the peace register.
reallocation.alternative_destination: M18 Hope, Expectation and Waiting — the VCG description ('inner being engaged through messianic hope') points toward M18 as equally valid. Researcher to confirm M33 vs M18.
reallocation.note: extracted_thin: only 2 verse records (1 active after Phase 2 set-aside). Analytical weight is very limited regardless of destination. Researcher may consider FLAG status.
